channel.title: AutoBe Blog
channel.description: Engineering notes, releases, and deep dives from AutoBE.

item.title: Qwen 3.5-27B Just Built Complete Backends from Scratch — 100% Compilation, 25x Cheaper
item.description: Qwen 3.5-27B generated complete backends with 100% compilation at 1/25th the cost of Claude Opus 4.6 — and the output quality is nearly identical. The benchmark proves it.

item.title: AutoBE vs. Claude Code: 3rd-gen coding agent developer's review of the leaked source code
item.description: We learned a great deal from the leaked Claude Code source code. What separates a 2nd-generation agent (Claude Code) from a 3rd-generation one (AutoBE), and where do they converge—this is a story of coexistence, not replacement.

item.title: Function Calling Harness: From 6.75% to 100%
item.description: 6.75% first-try function calling success becomes 100% compilation via type schemas, compilers, and structured feedback. Dissecting the harness engineering behind AutoBe and Typia.

item.title: [AutoBe] We Built an AI That Writes Full Backend Apps — Then Broke Its 100% Success Rate on Purpose
item.description: How we rebuilt AutoBE from a one-shot prototype builder to a modular development platform, recovering from 40% to near-100% compilation success through RAG optimization, weak-model testing, and type-driven validation.

item.title: [AutoBe] Achieved 100% compilation success of backend generation with \"qwen3-next-80b-a3b\"
item.description: AutoBE achieved 100% compilation success for backend applications using local AI models like qwen3-next-80b-a3b — a marked improvement from earlier attempts where most projects failed to build.

item.title: [AutoBe] Hardcore function calling benchmark in backend coding agent
item.description: AutoBE leverages LLM function calling throughout every phase rather than relying on plain text generation, including compiler AST structures with infinite depths — establishing the most extreme function calling benchmark ever.

item.title: [AutoBe] Built full-level backend applications with \"qwen-next-80b-a3b\" model
item.description: The AutoBE team has successfully tested the qwen3-next-80b-a3b-instruct model to generate three complete backend applications: To Do List, Reddit Community, and Economic Discussion Board.

item.title: Succeeded to build full-level backend application with \"qwen3-235b-a22b\" in AutoBE
item.description: AutoBE successfully generated a complete backend application without compilation errors using qwen3-235b-a22b model, consisting of 10 API functions and 37 DTO schemas.

item.title: [AutoBe] Qwen3-80B suddenly wrote doomsday AI mythology while generating a TODO app
item.description: During testing, the Qwen3-80B model unexpectedly generated approximately 3,000+ words of apocalyptic poetry instead of documentation while building a simple TODO application.

item.title: Built Reddit like community with AutoBe and AutoView (gpt-4.1-mini and qwen3-235b-a22b)
item.description: AutoBE demonstrated the ability to generate increasingly sophisticated backend applications beyond basic todo implementations using open-source and commercial AI models.

item.title: [AutoBE Hackathon] AI Chatbot generating Backend Application with AI Compilers ($6,400 Prize Pool)
item.description: Wrtn Technologies hosts the 1st AutoBE Hackathon — a coding competition focused on evaluating AI-generated backend applications with $6,400 in prizes.

item.title: [AutoBE] We made AI-friendly Compilers for Vibe Coding, achieving 100% build success
item.description: AutoBE employs a five-phase waterfall model with dedicated agents and specialized compilers — a Prisma compiler, OpenAPI validator, and TypeScript compiler — ensuring sequential quality assurance and 100% build success.

item.title: AutoBE, Backend No-Coding Agent Achieving 100% Compilation Success (Open Source)
item.description: AutoBE represents a revolutionary advancement in AI-assisted backend development. Rather than generating potentially flawed code snippets, AutoBE produces 100% working, production-ready backend applications through a compiler-driven approach.
